name: Fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2)

Oxygen/Gas total [Pure volume fraction] Inhaled gas

en.name: Fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2)
en.shortDescription: Fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2) — proportion of O2 in inspired gas, set or measured, in % or as a 0–1 fraction.
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2) is the proportion of oxygen in the gas a patient breathes in. Room air is about 21% oxygen; supplemental oxygen and ventilators deliver higher concentrations, up to 100%. In source data this is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp — either a ventilator/oxygen-device *setting* or a *measured* inspired oxygen concentration. It is recorded continuously in intensive care and anaesthesia for any patient receiving oxygen therapy or mechanical ventilation. It is reported either as a percentage (**%**, e.g. 40) or as a fraction between 0 and 1 (e.g. 0.40); the two scales differ by a factor of 100 [1]. ## Included Concepts The set lists the LOINC and SNOMED concepts for inspired oxygen concentration. These are leaf terms with no descendants, so they are listed explicitly rather than via a hierarchy anchor. They differ mainly by source (generic vs ventilator vs analyser) and by the scale used. ### Generic inspired oxygen concentration (default) Use these when the source records FiO2 without tying it to a specific ventilator or analyser. - *4353936 / 250774007 — Inspired oxygen concentration (SNOMED)*: the general observable for FiO2. Synonyms: "FIO2 - Inspired fraction of oxygen", "Inspired fraction of oxygen". - *3020716 / 3150-0 — Inhaled oxygen concentration (LOINC)*: generic inhaled oxygen concentration (%). ### Measured on / set by the ventilator - *3024882 / 19994-3 — Oxygen/Total gas setting [Volume Fraction] Ventilator (LOINC)*: the FiO2 *setting* on the ventilator (%). - *3026238 / 19996-8 — Oxygen/Inspired gas Respiratory system --on ventilator (LOINC)*: inspired oxygen in the respiratory system while on a ventilator (%). - *3025408 / 19995-0 — Oxygen/Inspired gas Respiratory system by O2 Analyzer --on ventilator (LOINC)*: as above, specifically measured by an inline oxygen analyser (%). ### Reported as a fraction (0–1) - *42869590 / 71835-3 — Oxygen/Gas total [Pure volume fraction] Inhaled gas (LOINC)*: FiO2 expressed as a pure volume fraction (a number between 0 and 1) rather than a percentage. **Note on units.** The recommended unit is **percent (`8554 / % — percent`, UCUM)** for every concept except *42869590 / 71835-3*, whose recommended unit is the dimensionless **fraction (`720868 / {fraction} — fraction`, UCUM)** — a number between 0 and 1 (LOINC created this term specifically for results reported as a decimal fraction without units [1]). A conversion is registered both ways: 1 fraction = 100 %, 1 % = 0.01 fraction. When harmonising, be explicit about the scale — a stored value of `0.4` on a `%` concept (meaning 0.4%) is almost certainly a fraction recorded under the wrong concept and should be 40%. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source records FiO2 without specifying the device: *4353936 / 250774007 — Inspired oxygen concentration*. - **Setting vs measured**: if the source distinguishes the FiO2 *set* on the ventilator from the *measured* inspired oxygen, map the setting to *3024882 / 19994-3 — Oxygen/Total gas setting [Volume Fraction] Ventilator* and the analyser-measured value to *3025408 / 19995-0*. If the distinction is not documented, use the generic concept. - **Scale matters**: values may arrive as a percentage (21–100) or as a fraction (0.21–1.0). Map to *42869590 / 71835-3* only when the source genuinely reports a 0–1 fraction; otherwise use a percent concept. Do not silently mix the two scales. - **Do not confuse FiO2 with related measurements**: inhaled oxygen *flow rate* (L/min) belongs to the Oxygen flow rate concept set; the PaO2/FiO2 ratio belongs to the Horowitz index concept set; arterial/blood oxygen content belongs to the blood-gas concept sets. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 3150-0 — Inhaled oxygen concentration. Available: https://loinc.org/3150-0/

versions.message: Add the pure-volume-fraction FiO2 concept (42869590 / 71835-3). Resolves to 6. Added clinical description.
